All those minds will truly be stable that are stable on God. They will be like Mount Zion, as firm as it is; a mountain supported by providence, even more so than a holy mountain supported by promise. They cannot be moved from confidence in God. They abide forever in that grace which is the guarantee of their everlasting continuance in glory. Committing themselves to God, they will be safe from their enemies. Even mountains may crumble and come to nothing, and rocks be removed, but God's covenant with his people cannot be broken, nor his care of them cease. Their troubles will last no longer than their strength will bear them up under them. The rod of the wicked may come, may fall upon the righteous, upon their persons, their estates, their liberties, their families, their names, on anything that falls to their lot; only it cannot reach their souls. And though it may come upon their lot, it will not rest there. The Lord will make all things work together for their good. The wicked will only prove a correcting rod, not a destroying sword; even this rod will not remain upon them, lest they distrust the promise, thinking God has cast them off.

God’s promises should encourage our prayers. The way of holiness is straight; there are no windings or changes in it. But the ways of sinners are crooked. They shift from one purpose to another, and turn here and there to deceive; but disappointment and misery will befall them. Those who cleave to the ways of God, though they may have trouble in their way, their end will be peace. The pleading of their Savior for them secures to them the upholding power and preserving grace of their God. Lord, number us with them, in time, and to eternity.
